benefitsPage.title: Your Benefits at a Glance
benefitsPage.description: Explore the complete range of benefits available to LEHR employees.

children.text: Our medical coverage helps maintain your wellbeing through preventive care, access to an extensive network of providers, and affordable prescription medication. Medical coverage is provided by UHC.
To see a current list of network providers online, visit www.myuhc.com.

With a Health Savings Account (HSA), contributions and withdrawals for qualified medical expenses are tax-free. Your HSA can be used for qualified expenses for you, your spouse and/or tax dependent(s), even if they are not covered by your plan.

If you are not enrolled in a HDHP but you have unused HSA funds from a previous account, those funds can still be used for qualified medical expenses. The annual maximum contribution limit to HSAs is set by the IRS each year.
2025 IRS Maximum Contributions: Individual $4,300 | Family $8,550 | Catch-Up (Ages 55+) $1,000.
You must be enrolled in the HDHP plan to contribute to a Health Savings Account (HSA).
LEHR offers affordable dental plan options for routine care and beyond. Coverage is available from UHC. Employees may purchase dental insurance on a voluntary basis.
Bi-weekly contributions for DPPO: Employee Only $14.10 | Employee + Spouse $27.89 | Employee + Child(ren) $38.87 | Employee + Family $57.26.
Employees may purchase vision insurance on a voluntary basis through UHC.
In-Network benefits: Exams $10 copay (every 12 months), Single Vision Lenses $25 copay, Bifocal $25 copay (every 12 months), Trifocal $25 copay, Frames $25 copay with $150 allowance.
Elective Contacts: $150 allowance in-network and out-of-network (every 12 months). Medically Necessary Contacts: Covered in full in-network, $210 allowance out-of-network.
Out-of-Network benefits: Exams $40 allowance, Single Vision Lenses $40 allowance, Bifocal $60 allowance, Trifocal $80 allowance, Frames $45 allowance.

LEHR provides employees with Basic Life and AD&D insurance as part of your basic coverage through Unum, which guarantees that your spouse or other designated survivor(s) continue to receive benefits after death.
Your Basic Life and AD&D insurance benefit is $10,000. If you are a full-time employee, you automatically receive Life and AD&D insurance even if you waive other coverage.
You may purchase Voluntary Life/AD&D insurance for yourself in $10,000 increments, up to a maximum of the lesser of 5x earnings or $500,000. Evidence of Insurability (EOI) is required over $100,000.
Voluntary Life/AD&D insurance is available in $5,000 increments for your spouse, up to the lesser of the employee amount or $250,000. EOI is required over $25,000.
You may purchase Voluntary Life/AD&D insurance in $2,000 increments for your child(ren), up to $10,000. EOI is not required for children.

LEHR offers disability coverage to protect you financially in the event you cannot work due to a debilitating illness or injury. A portion of your income is protected until you can return to work. Employees may purchase disability insurance on a voluntary basis.
Weekly Maximum Benefit: 60% of your income, up to $1,500. Elimination Period: 7 days. Maximum Benefit Period: 12 weeks.
Long-term disability coverage protects a portion of your income until you can return to work or you reach retirement age.

Monthly Maximum Benefit: 60% of your income, up to $6,500. Elimination Period: 90 days. Maximum Benefit Period: Payments last for as long as you are disabled or until you reach your Social Security Normal Retirement Age, whichever is sooner.
